name: Grand Shatranj
author: Joe Joyce (2006) / AGP port
description: Joe Joyce's 10x10 'what if chess pieces never became long-range sliders?' (2006). Every piece steps or jumps at most a few squares: the Jumping General (step 1 or leap 2 any direction), Minister (wazir + dabbabah + knight), High Priestess (ferz + alfil + knight), and two double-riders that move once or twice in a straight line -- the diagonal Oliphant and the orthogonal Lightning War Machine (1-4 squares, jumping over men). Pawns have no double step and promote only to lost pieces (optional on the 9th rank, mandatory on the 10th; a pawn stranded on the 10th shuffles sideways until a piece is lost). Win by checkmate or by baring the enemy king -- but an immediate counter-bare is a draw. This is the author's primary 'D' setup (War Machines in the corners, not rooks).
